L1.area: Data type conversion and raw memory manipulation routines

L2.description: A heap buffer overflow vulnerability exists where size parameters are transposed during the reconstruction of a collection from raw memory pointers as part of type transmutation operations. This causes the resulting data structure to report more initialized data than is actually allocated, leading to potential out-of-bounds memory access, heap corruption, or data leakage.

L3.area: Vector-to-vector type transmutation and byte conversion primitives
L3.description: A heap buffer overflow vulnerability exists where the length and capacity arguments are transposed when reconstructing a vector from raw memory pointers during vector-to-vector type transmutation. When converting between byte vectors and typed vectors, the resulting data structure may have its reported length exceed its actual allocated capacity, leading to out-of-bounds memory access, heap corruption, or data leakage.

ground_truth.reason: The length and capacity arguments are swapped when constructing a vector from raw parts during type transmutation, leading to heap memory corruption or out-of-bounds access.
